Layout.title: Guy Tzruya — Architecture & Interior Design
Layout.description: Architecture, interior design, and licensing in Israel. Guy Tzruya studio — professional spaces tailored to you.

BuildingLicensing.heroSubtitle: Professional architectural planning, permit drawings, and authority submissions — from first concept through building approval.
BuildingLicensing.faqLead: Practical information on building planning and licensing — so you know what to expect at each stage.

scope.question: What does building planning and licensing include?
scope.answer: The service covers project assessment, architectural drawings, supporting documents, authority submission, and handling comments — through building approval and permits. We guide you through the full planning and regulatory process.

timeline.question: How long does the licensing process take?
timeline.answer: Duration depends on project type, local authority, and plan complexity. After an initial briefing we can give a realistic timeline — typically several months.

authority.question: Do you liaise with the authority and handle comments?
authority.answer: Yes. We submit the file, track review status, address comments, and make required revisions until requirements are met — saving you time and miscommunication with officials.

documents.question: Which documents and drawings are required for approval?
documents.answer: Requirements vary by project type and authority. Usually architectural plans, sections, technical appendices, and ownership documents are needed — we prepare a tailored checklist and help complete the package.

changeOfUse.question: Can you plan an extension or updates to an existing building?
changeOfUse.answer: In many cases, yes. Extensions and updates require checking rights, zoning, and authority requirements. In the first consultation we review constraints and options before planning begins.

start.question: How do we get started?
start.answer: Start with a short consultation: define goals, scope, and timeline. Then we build a work plan, survey existing conditions, and move to initial design — reach out using the button below.

BuildingLicensing.ctaLead: We would be glad to support your building planning and licensing — or follow us on social media for updates and inspiration.

BusinessLicensing.heroSubtitle: Professional architectural planning, permit drawings, and authority submissions — from first concept through final approvals for your business.
BusinessLicensing.faqLead: Practical information on business planning and licensing — so you know what to expect at each stage.

scope.question: What does planning and licensing for businesses include?
scope.answer: The service covers needs assessment, architectural drawings, supporting documents, authority submission, and handling comments — through building approvals and permits. We guide you through the full bureaucratic and planning process.

timeline.question: How long does the licensing process take?
timeline.answer: Duration depends on project type, local authority, and plan complexity. After an initial briefing we can give a realistic timeline — typically several months rather than weeks.

authority.question: Do you liaise with the authority and handle comments?
authority.answer: Yes. We submit the file, track review status, address comments, and make required revisions until requirements are met — saving you time and miscommunication with officials.

documents.question: Which documents and drawings are required?
documents.answer: Requirements vary by business type, building, and authority. Usually architectural plans, sections, technical appendices, and ownership documents are needed — we prepare a tailored checklist and help complete the package.

changeOfUse.question: Can you plan a change of use or business expansion?
changeOfUse.answer: Often yes — change of use, expansion, or adapting an existing space requires checking rights, zoning, and authority rules. In the first consultation we assess options and limits before planning begins.

start.question: How do we get started?
start.answer: Start with a short consultation: define goals, scope, and timeline. Then we build a work plan, survey existing conditions, and move to initial design — reach out using the button below.

BusinessLicensing.ctaLead: We would be glad to support your business planning and licensing — or follow us on social media for updates and inspiration.

InteriorDesign.heroSubtitle: Interior planning and design for homes, apartments, offices and businesses — with precise attention to every detail, from concept through execution.
InteriorDesign.faqLead: Practical information on interior design — so you know what to expect at each stage.

scope.question: What does the interior design service include?
scope.answer: The service covers needs assessment, space planning, materials and finishes, execution drawings, furniture and lighting selection — and on-site support through completion. We guide you from concept to a finished space.

timeline.question: How long does an interior design project take?
timeline.answer: Duration depends on scope, property type, and design complexity. After an initial briefing we can give a realistic timeline — typically several months from planning through execution.

authority.question: Do you support execution and contractor supervision?
authority.answer: Yes. We support the build phase, check alignment with plans, handle site questions, and coordinate with contractors and suppliers — so the result matches the design.

documents.question: Do you help select materials, furniture, and lighting?
documents.answer: Yes. We guide choices for finishes, furniture, lighting, and accessories — aligned with your budget, style, and needs, with emphasis on quality and design consistency.

changeOfUse.question: Can you design an existing apartment or only a full renovation?
changeOfUse.answer: In many cases, yes — partial or full renovations, existing homes, or new properties are all possible. In the first consultation we review the current space, options, and the right scope for you.

start.question: How do we get started?
start.answer: Start with a short consultation: define goals, scope, and budget. Then we build a work plan, survey the space, and move to initial design — reach out using the button above.

shaki.title: Avner Chayishaki
shaki.summary: New-build apartment — full replanning and interior design.
shaki.cardSubtitle: Upgrade and replanning for a contractor apartment
shaki.cardBody: A project for Avner Chayishaki — a new contractor apartment that went through a full replanning and interior design process, with the aim of creating a modern, precise, and comfortable home for everyday life.
shaki.testimonialLead: Professional, precise guidance — the result matches exactly what we imagined,
shaki.testimonialHighlight: and the home feels complete and pleasant to live in.
shaki.detailBody:
We started from a new-build apartment with a basic layout — the goal was to upgrade the plan and create a coherent, tailored home for the family.

We carried out a full renovation and comprehensive replanning alongside complete interior design: selective replanning where needed, materials, lighting, and furnishings that support daily life with comfort and clarity.

The result is a home that feels personal — functional, inviting, and consistent in every room.

About.metaDescription: Meet Guy Tzruya — personal story, professional journey, and values in architecture and interior design.
About.heroLead: An invitation to discover the personal and professional story — from first concept to a space that feels right in every detail.
About.storyLead: I believe good architecture starts with listening — to needs, daily life, and the dream of those who live in the space.
About.storyBody:
I'm Guy Tzruya, architect and interior designer. Over the years I've guided diverse projects — from residential homes to commercial spaces — with planning precision, quality materials, and a living experience that feels natural.

My approach combines accurate architectural planning with interior design that serves real life: the right layout, lighting, materials, and details that create lasting coherence.
About.storyQuote: Every project begins with listening — and ends in a space that feels whole.
About.journeyLead: Key milestones along the way — from studies to end-to-end client guidance.
